back_translation: You shall not see the ox of your brother or his sheep straying, and ignore them; returning you shall return them to your brother.

And if your brother is not near to you, and you do not know him, then you shall gather it to the midst of your house, and it shall be with you until your brother seeks it, and you shall return it to him.
And thus you shall do to his donkey, and thus you shall do to his garment, and thus you shall do to every lost thing of your brother that he loses from him and you find; you cannot ignore it.
You shall not see the donkey of your brother or his ox falling on the way, and ignore them; raising you shall raise it with him.
There shall not be a man's gear on a woman, and a man shall not wear a woman's garment, for an abomination of the Lord your God is everyone who does these things.
When a bird's nest is found before you on the way, in any tree or on the ground, chicks or eggs, and the mother is sitting on the chicks or on the eggs, you shall not take the mother with the young.
Sending you shall send away the mother, and the young you shall take for yourself, so that it may be well with you and you may prolong days.
When you build a new house, you shall make a parapet for your roof, and you shall not put bloods on your house, when the falling one falls from it.
You shall not sow your vineyard with two kinds, lest it be set apart, the fullness of the seed that you sow, and the produce of the vineyard.
You shall not plow with an ox and with a donkey together.
You shall not wear mixed material, wool and linen together.
Tassels you shall make for yourself on the four corners of your covering with which you cover yourself.
When a man takes a wife, and he comes to her, and he hates her,
and puts to her charges of words, and brings out against her a bad name, and says: This woman I took, and I came to her, and I did not find for her virginity,
then the father of the young woman and her mother shall take, and they shall bring out the proof of virginity of the young woman to the elders of the city at the gate,
and the father of the young woman shall say to the elders: My daughter I gave to this man as a wife, and he hates her,

and behold, he places charges of words, saying: I did not find for your daughter virginity. And these are the proofs of virginity of my daughter. And they shall spread the cloth before the elders of the city.
And the elders of that city shall take the man, and they shall discipline him.

And they shall fine him a hundred of silver, and they shall give to the father of the young woman, because he brought out a bad name against a virgin of Israel; and to him she shall be as a wife, he shall not be able to send her away all his days.
But if true was this matter, no proofs of virginity were found for the young woman,

then they shall bring out the young woman to the door of her father's house, and the men of her city shall stone her with stones and she shall die, for she has done folly in Israel by playing the harlot in her father's house. And you shall purge the evil from your midst.
When a man is found lying with a woman married to a husband, both of them shall die, also both, the man lying with the woman and the woman; and you shall purge the evil from Israel.
When there is a young woman, a virgin, betrothed to a man, and a man finds her in the city, and lies with her,

then you shall bring out both of them to the gate of that city, and you shall stone them with stones and they shall die, the young woman because of the matter that she did not cry out in the city, and the man because of the matter that he afflicted the wife of his neighbor; and you shall purge the evil from your midst.
But if in the field a man finds the young woman who is betrothed, and the man overpowers her and lies with her, then the man who lay with her shall die alone.
And to the young woman, you shall not do a thing; there is to the young woman no sin worthy of death, for as when a man rises up against his neighbor and murders him, life, so is this matter.
For in the field he found her; the betrothed young woman cried out, and there was no one to save her.
When a man finds a young woman, a virgin, who is not betrothed, and he seizes her and lies with her, and they are found,

then the man who lay with her shall give to the father of the young woman fifty of silver, and to him she shall be as a wife, since he afflicted her; he shall not be able to send her away all his days.
